abstract: Multi-query attention (MQA) significantly accelerates decoder inference by utilizing a single key-value head; however, this efficiency often comes at the cost of model quality. Furthermore, the necessity of training a separate model specifically for faster inference is often impractical. In this work, we present two primary contributions: first, a streamlined recipe for "uptraining" existing multi-head language model checkpoints into MQA-capable models using only 5% of the original pre-training compute. Second, we introduce grouped-query attention (GQA), a versatile generalization of MQA that employs an intermediate number of key-value heads. Our results demonstrate that GQA achieves a performance profile nearly identical to multi-head attention while maintaining an inference speed comparable to MQA.
